definition: I2S satisfies one or more sequential literal CMPs (scheme, host/IP, HTTP/ framing, header tokens) inside curl's URL-parser and HTTP-response-parser, driving a complete transfer so downstream state conditions — byte counters, header-line counters, linked-list head pointers, socket event flags — become nonzero or non-null.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes 'HTTP/' and CRLF tokens from CMP buffer operands into the input, causing Curl_headers_push to populate the headers linked-list so the for-loop initializer sees a non-NULL head pointer.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 'HTTP/' and CRLF to populate curl headers linked list

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must encode a parseable HTTP response with CRLF-terminated headers so httphdrs.head is non-empty at cleanup.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must contain literal HTTP framing bytes (e.g. 'HTTP/' at the response position and CRLF '\r\n' line terminators) so Curl's header parser pushes entries onto data->state.httphdrs.head, making the head pointer non-NULL when Curl_headers_cleanup walks it.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner seeds carry the literal byte sequence 47 48 54 54 50 2f ('HTTP/') at offsets 0x24-0x29 followed by header text terminated by 0d 0a (CRLF); the W-only coverage of Curl_headers_push (12 hits) and namevalue (10 hits) confirms the parser actually populated the headers linked-list on the winner side, so the for-loop initializer e=httphdrs.head sees a non-NULL head.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds have random bytes at offsets 0x06-0x2c (no concentrated values; mostly %-characters, repeated 'e'/'Z', or short truncations); none of them carries 'HTTP/' literally or CRLF in the right positions, so Curl_headers_push and namevalue receive zero invocations on the L side and httphdrs.head stays NULL — the for-loop's first conditional evaluates false and the true branch is never taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The curl harness invokes integer/string CMPs against the literal 'HTTP/' (and the CRLF token) while parsing the synthetic response; cmplog's CmpLogObserver captures those operands and I2SRandReplace substitutes them into the input at matching offsets, planting the required literals far faster than naive's blind havoc; vpc inherits the same I2SRandReplace mutator, so it wins over plain value_profile for the same reason.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the for-loop initializer at headers.c:348 inside Curl_headers_cleanup, blocked side T.
Cmplog and vpc resolve every trial; naive and value_profile resolve zero of ten.
Both decisive pairs differ on the I2S technique only (cmplog vs naive, vpc vs value_profile).
Winners take the true branch and execute the loop body lines 349-352; losers reach the for-loop header but the body is dead.
The branch-flip is driven by whether the headers linked-list is populated: W enters the loop ten times with iteration count divergence T=10 F=30 vs L T=0 F=30.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 348  T=10 F=30  T=0 F=30  for(e = data->state.httphdrs.head; e; e = n) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Curl_headers_push (the function that appends to httphdrs.head) and namevalue (the header-line parser) are invoked only on the winner side.
All ten winner seeds carry the literal bytes 'HTTP/' at offsets 0x24-0x29 followed by a space and 'me' text.
Winner seed bytes at offsets 0x20-0x2f spell 'GHTTP/ mK@S' followed by CRLF and a header name, while losers have no such structure.
Loser seeds are short (32-38 bytes for four of five) and contain no 'HTTP/' literal — only random havoc bytes.
Cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of naive's mutator stack; the mutator scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other.
Value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and relies on CMP_MAP gradient feedback only.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a hand-crafted input that omits the literal 'HTTP/' and CRLF at the expected offsets still causes Curl_headers_push to populate httphdrs.head (e.g. because some other parser path on the harness appends a header), then non-NULL head at line 348 is not actually gated on those literals and the I2S-substitution story is wrong.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row floor confirms I2SRandReplace contributed somewhere in both winners' ancestor chains, but the dash rows sit 14-20 levels above the leaf — havoc on intermediate ancestors could be what actually flipped the gate, so we cannot prove that the specific 'HTTP/' bytes at 0x24-0x29 were planted by I2S rather than by lucky havoc on an I2S-seeded ancestor.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts multiple sequential CMPs (scheme 'pop3+-', host '127.0.0.1', 'GHTTP/') and substitutes each constant into fixed offsets, enabling a real HTTP transfer so the byte-count sum becomes positive at line 1708.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes scheme/host/HTTP literals to produce nonzero byte counters

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must encode a parseable harness scheme/host/port header so curl receives non-zero response bytes and bytecount+headerbytecount-deductheadercount > 0.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A multi-field harness preamble whose bytes at fixed offsets spell scheme/host/port/HTTP literals — specifically bytes 0x06-0x0b = 'pop3+-', bytes 0x0c-0x14 forming '127.0.0.1', bytes 0x24-0x29 spelling 'GHTTP/' — so the harness opens an HTTP-over-pop3 transfer that delivers a non-empty body, making (bytecount + headerbytecount - deductheadercount) > 0.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every cmplog-winning seed carries the exact literal byte sequences at the constrained offsets (0x06-0x0b='pop3+-' x10, 0x09='3' x10, 0x0a='+' x10, 0x0b='-' x10, 0x24-0x29='GHTTP/' x10), so the harness drives a real HTTP exchange whose byte counters become positive, taking the false branch at line 1708 into the return CURLE_OK path.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive/value_profile seeds at the same offsets show heavily scattered byte sets (e.g. 0x06: 43/31/33/41 +5 unique; 0x09: 25/47/2f/37 +4 unique; 0x24: 6f/41/4b/dc all singletons), no concentration on the literals — the harness rejects them, no bytes are read, bytecount stays 0, the (<=0) test holds, and execution enters the 'Empty reply from server' arm (lines 1714-1717) which is the L-only region.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace intercepts the harness's memcmp/strcmp against the scheme/host/port literals at fixed PCs, records (input_bytes, expected_constant) pairs into the CmpLogObserver buffer, then substitutes the expected constants at the matching input offsets in one mutation step. value_profile_cmplog inherits the same I2SRandReplace mutator and the same advantage over plain value_profile, which has only CMP_MAP distance gradients and no operand-substitution mutator and so cannot synthesize the multi-byte literals reliably within 12h.

evidence_trail.claim: The branch tests whether the running HTTP transfer received any payload bytes; the false (winner) side means bytecount+headerbytecount-deductheadercount > 0.
The true (loser) side leads to the 'Empty reply from server' failure path, which is the L-only region in the per-role overlay.
All 10 cmplog winners pin offsets 0x06-0x0b to the literal bytes 'pop3+-'.
Winner offset 0x09 is uniformly '3' across all 10 seeds; loser is scattered.
Winner offsets 0x24-0x29 spell 'GHTTP/' across all 10 seeds; loser bytes are all singletons.
Winner seeds carry the literal bytes '127.0.0.1' and 'HTTP/' in their hex+ASCII dumps.
All 10 cmplog trials resolved the branch while all 10 naive trials remained blocked for the full 12h budget — a complete probability divergence with a 545-hit per-branch hitcount gap.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=0/10  blocked=10  unreached=0
  avg duration blocked: winner=0.35h  loser=12.00h
  avg hitcount on branch: winner=545  loser=0
  prob_div=1.00  dur_div=11.65h  hit_div=545

evidence_trail.claim: The value_profile_cmplog vs value_profile pair shows the same complete probability divergence under the same I2S delta.

cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads CmpLogObserver entries, finds the input bytes matching one operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets — the exact mechanism that would imprint scheme/HTTP literals onto corpus seeds.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks the I2SRandReplace mutator and only has CMP_MAP gradient feedback, which cannot synthesize multi-byte literal substrings directly.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only gate is a multi-byte equality CMP against a fixed literal (e.g. memcmp(input+6,"pop3+-",6)==0) in which cmplog does NOT outperform naive at 12h — would falsify the I2S-substitution-of-scheme-literals account; or, conversely, observing that the cmplog winners' ancestor chains never contain a dash row would refute the lineage attribution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The mapping from each constrained byte offset to a specific harness memcmp/strcmp call site is inferred from the byte-pattern shapes (scheme name, IP address, 'HTTP/' literal) rather than from a direct read of the curl harness source, so a fraction of the constrained bytes may be gated by a different mechanism than I2S literal substitution.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace captures the 'HTTP/' literal from CMP operands logged during protocol-prefix parsing and substitutes it into the input, enabling sscanf to parse the status line and increment k->headerline so the false branch fires on subsequent iterations.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 'HTTP/' so sscanf increments headerline past zero

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a complete header line ending in 0x0a whose first line matches a recognized HTTP/RTSP protocol prefix so headerline counter increments.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The buffer must contain a newline byte (0x0a) AND the accumulated first header line must begin with a recognizable protocol prefix (literally the ASCII string 'HTTP/' or 'RTSP/') so that checkprotoprefix/checkhttpprefix returns non-STATUS_BAD, then sscanf(" HTTP/%1d.%1d%c%3d%c", ...) parses it and k->headerline is post-incremented at line 4298 (`if(!k->headerline++)`). Once headerline becomes non-zero, subsequent iterations take the false branch of `if(!k->headerline)` at line 3932 (the blocker).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries the literal ASCII bytes 'GHTTP/' at offsets 0x24-0x29 followed by ' ' and a status-line tail; the prefix 'HTTP/' starting at offset 0x25 matches the sscanf format string ' HTTP/%1d.%1d%c%3d%c' that sets nc>=4 and lets Curl_http_statusline succeed, after which headerline is incremented. The W set also exhibits a stable network/URL preamble (e.g. '127.0.0.1:9021') and trailing '\r\n' separators that produce well-formed header lines.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds (naive) lack the literal 'HTTP/' / 'RTSP/' substring at any aligned offset — at offset 0x24-0x29 W has 47 48 54 54 50 2f ('GHTTP/') while L shows a scatter of unrelated bytes (6f, 41, 4b, dc, ...). Without that 5-byte literal, checkprotoprefix returns STATUS_BAD on the first complete line and k->headerline is never incremented, so the (!k->headerline) test at line 3932 stays true on every subsequent partial-line iteration.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's CmpLogObserver records the literal 'HTTP/' (and the related multi-byte tokens inside sscanf / strcmp inside checkprotoprefix) as a CMP operand pair when the harness drives the parser; I2SRandReplace then substitutes that literal at random offsets in the input, manufacturing seeds that satisfy the protocol-prefix gate. value_profile_cmplog wins for the same reason — it has I2SRandReplace too. naive and value_profile have only havoc/token mutators with no I2S substitution, so finding the exact 5-byte 'HTTP/' literal at the right alignment is left to blind byte mutation.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker branch is `if(!k->headerline)` at http.c:3932 and the globally blocked side is the false branch.
naive and value_profile never resolve this branch across 10 trials each; cmplog and value_profile_cmplog resolve it in all 10 trials.
Both decisive pairs share the I2S technique delta.
Pair 2 is value_profile_cmplog > value_profile under the same I2S delta.
The headerline counter is post-incremented at line 4298 by `if(!k->headerline++)`, which is only reached after a complete header line is processed and the protocol prefix check succeeds.
Curl_http_readwrite_headers reaches sscanf with the format string ' HTTP/%1d.%1d%c%3d%c' to parse the first header line.

When a complete line is not yet present (no 0x0a in buffer), the loser branch executes checkprotoprefix on the partial buffer at line 3934-3937 — and L is the only side that hits these lines in the per-role overlay.
The W set always contains the literal ASCII 'GHTTP/' starting at offset 0x24 across all 5 displayed winning seeds.
At the immediately following offset 0x25 the W set always carries 0x48 ('H'), while L's bytes at that offset are random.
Offsets 0x26, 0x27, 0x28, 0x29 carry 'T','T','P','/' in all 10 winner samples — i.e. the full 'HTTP/' literal at fixed alignment.
The actual seed hexdump confirms 'GHTTP/' at row 0x20 of Seed 1 winner.
cmplog mutators include I2SRandReplace which substitutes CMP operands (such as 'HTTP/') into the input at scanned offsets.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and uses only havoc + token mutators, so it has no mechanism for substituting the 'HTTP/' literal directly.
value_profile_cmplog has I2SRandReplace, matching the cmplog mechanism for this branch.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only gate is matching a literal multi-byte string at a fixed input offset shows naive and value_profile reaching the gate at the same rate as cmplog and value_profile_cmplog within the duration budget — i.e. lacks_I2S fuzzers solve the literal-substitution gate without I2S help.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I inferred the gate is checkprotoprefix recognizing 'HTTP/' from the seed bytes plus the source layout, but I did not directly observe checkprotoprefix's internal string compare; an unrelated downstream condition that also requires 'HTTP/' (e.g. the sscanf format string at line 4319) could be the proximate CMP source recorded by CmpLogObserver, in which case the upstream blocker mechanism is the same I2S substitution but the exact CMP-site attribution is approximate.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the 5-byte 'HTTP/' literal from the CMP buffer into the input, letting sscanf match the HTTP version pattern and increment k->headerline so subsequent iterations take the false branch of the headerline check.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes 'HTTP/' so headerline increments and false branch fires

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain the literal ASCII bytes 'HTTP/' at the start of a header line so sscanf parses a status line and increments k->headerline.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A complete first header line that begins with the 5-byte ASCII literal 'HTTP/' (0x48 0x54 0x54 0x50 0x2f) so that sscanf(HEADER1, " HTTP/%1d.%1d%c%3d%c", ...) at line 4318 returns nc>=4 and Curl_http_statusline() is called, which causes k->headerline to be incremented past 0 by the !k->headerline++ branch at line 4298. Subsequent iterations then take the FALSE side of the blocker at line 3971.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds contain the exact byte sequence 'HTTP/' at offsets 0x25-0x29 (47 48 54 54 50 2f / 'GHTTP/'), so the sscanf at line 4318 matches the HTTP version pattern, nc>=4, k->httpcode is parsed, and the !k->headerline++ branch at line 4298 fires the first time, leaving k->headerline=1 for the second pass — flipping the line-3971 test to FALSE.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All five loser-blocking seeds carry 'From:' (46 72 6f 6d 3a) at offsets 0x25-0x29 instead of 'HTTP/', so sscanf returns nc=0 (no HTTP/ prefix), the else-if(!nc) checkhttpprefix path also fails (no 'HTTP' anywhere usable), and on subsequent loop iterations k->headerline remains 0, so the line-3971 test stays TRUE and the false branch is never taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace mutator reads the runtime CMP operands logged when sscanf's internal byte comparisons (and the Curl_dyn_addn / strcmp callbacks) compare an input span against the literal 'HTTP/', then substitutes that 5-byte literal directly into the input. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they would have to land all five bytes 'H','T','T','P','/' contiguously by havoc — a ~1/2^40 random-byte event per offset — which neither achieved in 10/10 trials over ~12h. The value_profile-only path has no equivalent single-step substitution and cannot synthesize the 5-byte literal in one shot; only I2SRandReplace does.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the false side of `if(!k->headerline)` at http.c:3971, reached inside the per-header-line do/while loop of Curl_http_readwrite_headers.
k->headerline is incremented by the post-increment in the line-4298 branch, which is what flips the line-3971 test to false on subsequent iterations.

The branch on the line-4298 increment path runs sscanf against the literal HTTP version pattern, so an input matching 'HTTP/%1d.%1d%c%3d%c' is what makes nc>=4 and clears the path to k->headerline incrementing.

Winners cleanly carry the literal 'HTTP/' (0x48 0x54 0x54 0x50 0x2f) at offsets 0x25-0x29 across all sampled seeds, while losers carry 'From:' at the same offsets — exactly the bytes the sscanf needs to start parsing a status line.
The second byte of the 'HTTP/' run is also a unanimous winner vs loser divergence (0x54 'T' x10 vs 0x72 'r' x9 + 0x01 x1), confirming the literal is the discriminating feature.
A representative winning seed shows the 'HTTP/' literal in situ at offset 0x24 of the seed bytes: the 'G' header tag (0x47) is followed immediately by 'HTTP/' (0x48 0x54 0x54 0x50 0x2f).
A representative loser-blocking seed carries 'From:' (46 72 6f 6d 3a) at the same offset where winners carry 'HTTP/' — no HTTP version prefix present.

The blocker line-3971 is reached on both W and L sides ([B] line 3971), but only W proceeds past it to lines 4000+ that depend on k->headerline being already set — confirming the divergence is at the line-3971 test direction, not at reachability.
Curl_http_statusline is invoked 10 times in W and zero times in L — the natural consequence of W's sscanf matching the HTTP/ prefix and L's failing.

On the W side, the !k->headerline++ branch at line 4298 took the true side 10 times (executing the status-line parsing block); on the L side it never fired — direct evidence the loser never increments k->headerline.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4298  T=10 F=12  T=0 F=0  if(!k->headerline++) {

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs are I2S deltas (cmplog>naive and value_profile_cmplog>value_profile), so the technique axis present in winners and absent in losers is specifically I2SRandReplace.
value_profile_cmplog vs value_profile is the other decisive pair on the same I2S axis — winners 10/10 resolve, losers 10/10 block.

Mechanism context attributes the I2S substitution to cmplog's I2SRandReplace mutator, which scans the input for one operand of an intercepted CMP and substitutes the other — exactly the operation that would inject the literal 'HTTP/' at a CMP site.
naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace (only havoc + token mutators), which explains why they cannot synthesize the 5-byte literal in one step.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the gate at line 3971 is reachable but the operand it depends on is NOT a literal byte sequence in the input — e.g., k->headerline is incremented based on a runtime-computed value the input bytes do not directly equal — should show cmplog and naive equally slow. If, on such a harness, cmplog still resolved 10/10 while naive blocked, then I2SRandReplace literal-substitution is NOT the mechanism here and the hypothesis is refuted.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff shows the 'HTTP/' literal is present in winners and absent in losers, but does not directly prove the CMP that I2S intercepted was the sscanf-internal one (vs an earlier strncmp/memcmp in checkhttpprefix or Curl_dyn_addn); the precise CMP site that fired I2SRandReplace is inferred from source rather than logged.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace copies multiple protocol literals (scheme 'pop3+-', host, port, 'HTTP/', header keywords) from CMP buffer into fixed input offsets, traversing all upstream gates to reach multi_done with result==CURLE_OK and rc nonzero.
signature.one_line: I2S serially substitutes protocol literals to satisfy all upstream curl gates

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a parseable protocol scheme, IP:port, HTTP request line, and header tokens so a full transfer reaches multi_done with non-zero Curl_pgrsDone return.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structured byte sequence containing several literal multi-byte tokens at fixed offsets: a protocol scheme prefix 'pop3+-' at 0x06-0x0b, a dotted-quad host 0x0c-0x14 ('127.0.0.1' / '.0.0.1:'), an ASCII port string near 0x15-0x1a ('9021' or similar), and an HTTP request-line/header fragment from 0x24 onwards containing 'HTTP/' and recognised header names like 'Retry-A' or 'TranSfe'. All five winner seeds share these literal strings; together they drive the harness through scheme parsing, connect, HTTP transfer, and into multi_done with result==CURLE_OK while Curl_pgrsDone returns non-zero, satisfying '!result && rc'.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries the literal byte runs 'pop3+-' at 0x06-0x0b, '127.0.0.1' at 0x0c-0x14, ':9021'/'9021' at 0x16-0x1a, 'GHTTP/' at 0x24-0x29 and header keywords (e.g. 'Retry-A', 'TranSfe') at 0x2f+. Cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace observes these constants in curl's strcmp/memcmp/strncmp calls during URL/protocol/header parsing and pastes them into the input, unblocking each successive gate so execution reaches multi.c:654 and exercises the true branch (winner W(T/F)=10/0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds are short (35-47 bytes) random-byte / percent-encoding garbage at the same offsets (e.g. 0x06=%, 0x08-0x0a=random bytes, no 'pop3', no '127.0.0.1', no 'HTTP/'). Without the multi-byte literal scheme/host/HTTP tokens at the gates upstream of multi_done, naive's and value_profile's havoc mutators cannot synthesise the exact constants in tractable time: naive has no CMP introspection, and value_profile's CMP_MAP only rewards Hamming-distance progress per-CMP, which scales poorly across multiple independent multi-byte equality gates that must all be satisfied in sequence to reach multi_done.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace. Cmplog and value_profile_cmplog intercept the integer/string CMPs in curl's URL parser, scheme dispatch, hostname resolver, port parser, and HTTP header tokenizer; I2SRandReplace then copies those concrete constants into the seed at offsets where the input byte appeared as one CMP operand. value_profile alone cannot perform substitution (it only buckets Hamming distance), so it stays blocked just like naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ on the I2S axis; cmplog and value_profile_cmplog resolve every trial while naive and value_profile resolve at most 1/10 and 0/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive                            1        9          0  loser (I2S vs cmplog)
cmplog                          10        0          0  winner (I2S vs naive)
value_profile                    0       10          0  loser (I2S vs value_profile_cmplog)
value_profile_cmplog            10        0          0  winner (I2S vs value_profile)

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 1 (vpc>vp) and Pair 2 (cmp>naive) both carry the I2S technique delta, supporting a single-feature collapse.
The blocker fires on a compound predicate combining the result of conn->handler->done with the progress-callback return; the path to it traverses several conditional gates from line 612 onward.
Winner-resolving seeds share the literal protocol-scheme byte run 'pop3+-' at offsets 0x06-0x0b.
Winner-resolving seeds share the literal IP:port substring '127.0.0.1:9021' or '127.0.0.1:9001' starting at 0x0d.
Winner-resolving seeds share the literal 'HTTP/' token at offsets 0x24-0x29 and HTTP-header keywords 'TranSfe' / 'Retry-A' at 0x30+.
Loser-blocking seeds carry no protocol-scheme or HTTP literals at those offsets; instead they hold random '%'-percent-encoding noise.

At every byte offset spanning the scheme/host/port/HTTP region (0x05 through 0x2f), W bytes are a concentrated single-value set (e.g. 70(p)x10, 33(3)x10, 47(G)x10, 48(H)x10, 54(T)x10) while L bytes are a high-cardinality random set — the textbook fingerprint of I2S substituting a fixed constant.
At offset 0x0024 ten of ten winner seeds carry 'G' while loser seeds show four distinct random bytes — the boundary of the literal 'GHTTP/' constant.
The d=1 row at line 654 confirms the direction split: winners take T=10 F=0, losers take T=0 F=10 — perfect role separation on the blocker itself.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 654  T=10 F=0  T=0 F=10  if(!result && rc)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's mechanism is I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for byte sequences matching one CMP operand and substitutes the other — exactly the pattern observed for 'pop3+-', '127.0.0.1', and 'HTTP/'.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Mutators**: naive's havoc + token stack **plus** `I2SRandReplace`.
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile does NOT have I2SRandReplace — it only updates a Hamming-distance CMP_MAP — which is why vp blocks just like naive on this branch.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic experiment that replaces the literal multi-byte constants 'pop3+-', '127.0.0.1', '9021', 'HTTP/', and the header keywords with runtime-derived (non-literal) operands — e.g. a checksum-gated dispatcher — and observes cmplog/vpc still resolving the branch within the same wallclock as naive/vp would refute the I2S-substitution explanation. Equivalently: if removing the literal tokens from the winner seeds (replacing them with random bytes of the same length) and replaying still resolves the branch, the hypothesis is wrong.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The chain from the harness entry to multi.c:654 involves function-pointer dispatch (conn->handler->done) which the source-grep callers index does not fully resolve, so the exact set of CMP sites upstream that I2S substitutes is inferred from the seed byte patterns rather than confirmed by an annotated call chain.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace plants 'pop3+-' at the scheme offset and 'HTTP/' at the response offset from CMP operands, driving curl through a real read/write exchange so bytecount+headerbytecount becomes nonzero and the false branch fires at line 1866.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes scheme and response literals to produce nonzero byte counts

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must encode a complete URL scheme plus a parseable HTTP-like server response so the request actually receives bytes and bytecount+headerbytecount != 0.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must contain (a) a recognized URL-scheme literal at the front (winners carry 'pop3+-' at offsets 0x06-0x0b, an extended pop3 form curl's URL parser accepts) followed by a host:port spec like '127.0.0.1:9021', AND (b) a synthetic server-response payload at offsets 0x20-0x2b that begins with the literal 'HTTP/' so curl's response parser accepts framing bytes and increments data->req.bytecount/headerbytecount, making the sum at line 1866 non-zero (the FALSE side).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner seeds share the literal byte sequence 70 6f 70 33 2b 2d ('pop3+-') at offsets 0x06-0x0b and 47 48 54 54 50 2f ('GHTTP/') at offsets 0x24-0x29, which drives the request through the read/write data path: HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE shows readwrite_upload, Curl_fillreadbuffer, Curl_done_sending, and Curl_http2_done_sending all fire on W with zero L hits, and DIVERGENT BRANCHES confirms W takes the FALSE side ten times at L1866 (T=0 F=10), meaning bytecount+headerbytecount evaluated > 0.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are short and unstructured (32-38 bytes for four of five) with no concentrated bytes at 0x06-0x0b (variants 31 5a 2b 25 41, no 'pop3+-' literal) and no 'HTTP/' literal anywhere in 0x24-0x29 (L bytes there are 6f/41/4b/dc — random havoc residue), so the URL never resolves to a valid connection and no response bytes are read; DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows L takes TRUE every time at L1866 (T=10 F=0) because bytecount+headerbytecount stays at zero, and HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE shows zero L invocations of Curl_fillreadbuffer and readwrite_upload.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: curl's URL parser performs literal string CMPs against scheme names ('pop3', '+', '-' suffix characters) and its response parser performs a literal CMP against 'HTTP/' at the start of an incoming buffer; cmplog's CmpLogObserver captures both pairs of operands and I2SRandReplace substitutes them into the input at matching offsets, planting 'pop3+-' at 0x06 and 'HTTP/' at 0x24 in one mutation rather than blindly stumbling onto 11 consecutive ASCII bytes. value_profile_cmplog inherits the same I2SRandReplace mutator on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback, which is why both vpc>vp and cmplog>naive are decisive on the I2S axis.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the bytecount+headerbytecount==0 conjunct at transfer.c:1866 column 6 inside Curl_retry_request; the blocked side is FALSE.
Globally blocked side is FALSE, meaning the winner needs to take the FALSE branch (sum is non-zero).
Cmplog and value_profile_cmplog resolve all ten trials; naive and value_profile resolve zero of ten.
Both decisive pairs differ on the I2S technique only (cmplog vs naive, value_profile_cmplog vs value_profile).
The second decisive pair (vpc>vp) is also a single-axis I2S difference.
At L1866 the winner side takes the false branch ten times and the loser takes the true branch ten times — a clean directional flip on this exact branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1866  T=0 F=10  T=10 F=0  if((data->req.bytecount + data->req.headerbytecount == 0) &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The read/upload data-path functions fire only on the winner side, confirming the winner actually received/transferred bytes.
Curl_fillreadbuffer (the function that drains response bytes into the request buffer) is W-only.
All ten winner seeds share the literal bytes 'pop3+-' at offsets 0x06-0x0b (concentrated single-value sets per offset).
Offset 0x0a is consistently '+' on W and scattered random bytes on L — characteristic of an I2S-substituted scheme separator.
All ten winner seeds carry the literal bytes 'GHTTP' at offsets 0x24-0x28 (single-value W sets, scattered L sets).
Winner seed 1 contains the full 'HTTP/' literal at bytes 0x24-0x29 followed by space and 'mK@Sn' header text.
Winner seed 2 contains the 'pop3+-' scheme literal at offsets 0x06-0x0b and a host:port string '127.0.0.1' starting at 0x0d.
Loser seeds are short (32-38 bytes) and carry no 'HTTP/' or 'pop3+-' literal — example loser seed 1 ends at 32 bytes with random havoc bytes.
Cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of naive's havoc+token stack — the mutator that scans input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other.
Value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and relies on CMP_MAP gradient feedback only, which explains why it loses to value_profile_cmplog on this branch.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a hand-crafted input that omits the literal 'HTTP/' at offsets 0x24-0x29 and uses an arbitrary 4-byte placeholder there still drives bytecount+headerbytecount above zero (e.g. because some other code path on the harness increments those counters independently of the response parser), then 'HTTP/' literal substitution is not the required gate and the I2S-substitution mechanism story is wrong.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row floor confirms an I2SRandReplace contribution somewhere in the seed's 21-level ancestor chain, but the dash rows sit at depths 19-20 (near the root); we cannot mechanically prove that the specific 'HTTP/' bytes at 0x24-0x29 in the leaf came from I2S rather than from lucky havoc on an I2S-seeded ancestor, nor can we distinguish whether the 'pop3+-' scheme literal or the 'HTTP/' response literal is the dominant I2S target.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts curl's scheme strcmp against 'pop3' (and HTTP/URL tokens) and substitutes those literals into the input, enabling the upload/connect path where the writefd revents false branch is reachable.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes 'pop3' scheme bytes to unlock upload/socket code path

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must encode a parseable pop3-style URL header so curl reaches the poll/socket-check path with an upstream socket pending.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structured fuzz-input whose protocol-scheme bytes spell 'pop3' at offsets 0x06-0x09 and whose subsequent bytes form a host:port URL (e.g. '127.0.0.1:9001') plus an HTTP-style request line ('GHTTP/' at 0x24-0x29) — i.e. the input matches curl's expected protocol/URL string CMPs so the fuzzer harness drives curl into the upload/connect path where Curl_socket_check is invoked with a writefd socket whose revents come back WITHOUT (POLLRDNORM|POLLIN|POLLERR|POLLHUP), taking the false side of the readfd0 revents mask test.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed contains the literal ASCII 'pop3' (70 6f 70 33) at offsets 0x06-0x09 and a CRLF-terminated URL+HTTP-header payload (`127.0.0.1:9001`, `GHTTP/`, `mewhere\r\n`). HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE confirms the winners enter the upload path (Curl_get_upload_buffer 20 hits, ossl_seed/ossl_random 20 hits, Curl_fillreadbuffer 10, Curl_done_sending 10, readwrite_upload 10) — all zero for the loser. At branch L231 the winner takes both T (14) and F (10); the loser only ever takes T (10) and never F.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are 32-38 bytes of garbled bytes with no 'pop3' magic at 0x06-0x09 (offsets show 1Z`Gee^, p3:0/12, A22%e8, +-k7%E, %AAA — random havoc). They never satisfy curl's scheme strcmp, so the harness never wires up the upload path; Curl_socket_check is only ever called on a readfd path where revents arrives with POLLIN/POLLRDNORM set (true side), never on the writefd path where the false side becomes reachable.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace observes the strcmp/memcmp CMP_MAP entries when curl parses the scheme ('pop3', 'http://', host, port) and substitutes those literal operands into the input at the matching offsets. Naive lacks CMP interception entirely (per MECHANISM CONTEXT, 'No I2S'), so it must brute-force every byte of the protocol header by havoc, which is statistically infeasible inside the 12h budget — naive blocks 10/10 trials while cmplog resolves 10/10 within ~66-3064s. The lineage of two winning seeds (1abae053b6f68107, 06c4f49e154580bb) both terminate in I2S-floor dash rows (depth 7-8 and 16-17 respectively), directly confirming I2SRandReplace contribution.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is at curl/lib/select.c:231 in Curl_socket_check, false side globally blocked.
evidence_trail.cited_locator: Location and Globally blocked side lines
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Location: /src/curl/lib/select.c:231:8
Enclosing function: Curl_socket_check
Source line:     if(pfd[num].revents & (POLLRDNORM|POLLIN|POLLERR|POLLHUP))
Globally blocked side: F

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog resolves 10/10 while naive blocks 10/10 with the largest possible prob_div=1.0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=0/10  blocked=10  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is the single canonical I2S delta (cmplog vs naive).
Every winner-resolving seed contains the literal ASCII 'pop3' bytes at offsets 0x06-0x09.
Winning seeds embed a host:port URL fragment '127.0.0.1:9001'.
Winning seeds embed an HTTP-style request line and CRLF-terminated header.
BYTE DIFF flags offsets 0x06-0x09 as a strong magic-bytes divergence: all 10 winners hold 70/6f/70 and 6f/33; loser bytes are 10+ distinct values.
Loser-blocking seeds are short (32-38 bytes) garbled inputs that do not contain the pop3 scheme.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Seed 1 (id=0022f5056b29d1a1, size=32 bytes, fuzzer=naive, trial=1, discovered_at=73s, mutation_op=BytesInsertMutator,BytesDeleteMutator,QwordAddMutator,QwordAddMutator):
  0000: 00 01 00 00 00 19 31 5a 60 47 65 65 5e 25 5a 5a

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker line the winner takes both T and F directions while the loser only ever takes T and never F.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 231  T=14 F=10  T=10 F=0  if(pfd[num].revents & (POLLRDNORM|POLLIN|POLLERR|POLLHUP))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner exclusively enters the upload/TLS path (Curl_get_upload_buffer, ossl_seed, ossl_random, readwrite_upload, etc.) while the loser hits zero of those — proof the loser never advances past curl's protocol-scheme parsing.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
20         0  Curl_get_upload_buffer  (/src/curl/lib/transfer.c:118-125)
      20         0  openssl.c:ossl_seed  (/src/curl/lib/vtls/openssl.c:807-883)
      20         0  openssl.c:ossl_random  (/src/curl/lib/vtls/openssl.c:4438-4451)
      16         0  Curl_single_getsock  (/src/curl/lib/transfer.c:1352-1387)
      16         0  openssl.c:rand_enough  (/src/curl/lib/vtls/openssl.c:802-804)
      10         0  Curl_fillreadbuffer  (/src/curl/lib/transfer.c:163-363)
      10         0  Curl_done_sending  (/src/curl/lib/transfer.c:882-896)
      10         0  transfer.c:readwrite_upload  (/src/curl/lib/transfer.c:928-1154)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's mutator set includes I2SRandReplace, which scans input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack **plus** `I2SRandReplace`.
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S — by mechanism it cannot perform operand substitution.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) in cmplog/vpc lineage are direct lower-bound evidence of I2SRandReplace finds.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
silent ParentInfo-only entries
(`mutation_op = -` in lineage output) that — in cmplog/vpc only —
indicate an I2SRandReplace find under the current build. The dash
rows are exclusive to cmplog and value_profile_cmplog; their
presence in a winning seed's ancestor chain is direct (lower-bound)
evidence of I2S contribution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only winning constraint is a 4-byte literal 'pop3' equality CMP at a fixed input offset should show cmplog>>naive at scan_value=4 bytes; if naive resolves it within the same budget (e.g. <=2x cmplog's median time-to-resolve) then the 4-byte magic-string gate is NOT what blocks naive here and the mechanism would be refuted.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly read the curl fuzz harness source to confirm the 'pop3'+URL+HTTP-line layout is exactly what it parses; the input-feature description is inferred from BYTE DIFF concentration and HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE rather than from the harness code.
